common_patterns.overview: Common bug patterns and review heuristics to scan for during code review. These are recurring patterns that frequently indicate problems.

pattern.name: null_undefined_handling
pattern.description: Missing guards against null or undefined values

signals.signal: Property access on a value that could be null/undefined without optional chaining (?.)
Function parameters used without default values or guards when callers may omit them
API response destructuring without checking the response shape
Array methods (.map, .filter, .find) called on potentially undefined variables

pattern.name: async_race_conditions
pattern.description: Concurrent async operations that can interfere with each other

signals.signal: Shared mutable state accessed from multiple async functions without synchronization
useEffect with async operations that don't clean up (missing abort controller, missing cancelled flag)
Multiple setState calls that depend on previous state without using the functional updater form
Promise.all on independent operations where one failure aborts others unnecessarily
await inside a loop that could be parallelized (or vice versa — parallel when sequential is needed)

pattern.name: error_swallowing
pattern.description: Errors caught but not properly handled or reported

signals.signal: Empty catch blocks: `catch (e) { }` or `catch { }`
catch blocks that only log without re-throwing or handling the error state
`.catch(() => {})` on promises without any handling
try/finally without catch — cleanup runs but errors propagate silently in some contexts

pattern.name: state_stale_closure
pattern.description: Stale closures capturing outdated state in React hooks or callbacks

signals.signal: useCallback or useEffect referencing state/props without including them in the dependency array
setTimeout/setInterval referencing state that may have changed by the time they fire
Event listeners attached in useEffect that capture initial state and never update
useRef used to work around stale closures without a comment explaining why

pattern.name: resource_leaks
pattern.description: Resources that are acquired but never released

signals.signal: setInterval without clearInterval in a cleanup function
Event listeners added without corresponding removeEventListener
Database connections or file handles opened but not closed
WebSocket or SSE connections without close/disconnect in cleanup
Observable subscriptions without unsubscribe

pattern.name: test_assertion_quality
pattern.description: Tests that appear to test something but don't actually verify behavior

signals.signal: `expect(true).toBe(true)` or other tautological assertions
Tests with no assertions at all (only setup code)
Assertions that only check for existence (`expect(result).toBeDefined()`) without verifying values
Mock functions that are set up but never asserted against
Tests that only test mock behavior, not actual integration

pattern.name: hardcoded_values
pattern.description: Values that should be configurable or derived but are hardcoded

signals.signal: API URLs, keys, or secrets hardcoded in source files
Magic numbers without named constants or explanatory comments
Timeout values, retry counts, or thresholds duplicated across files
Environment-specific values that should come from env vars or config

pattern.name: missing_edge_cases
pattern.description: Code that handles the happy path but not edge cases

signals.signal: Functions that don't handle empty arrays, empty strings, zero, or negative numbers
User input handling without trimming, validation, or sanitization
API calls that don't handle 4xx/5xx responses or network failures
Pagination or list rendering without handling the empty state
File operations without handling permission errors or missing files
